## Done. Here's what shipped:

**Instagram → Supabase** ✅
- Stored in `ej_site_config` (`key: "content_overrides"`, `value: { instagram: "https://www.instagram.com/depiflash.py" }`)
- Both footer and contacto page read from content (via `content.instagram`) — no more hardcoding

**Content overrides system** ✅
- `/api/content` — public GET, returns Supabase overrides merged over es.json
- `/api/admin/content` — admin PUT, writes overrides to Supabase
- `lib/content-provider.tsx` — React context that fetches overrides, deep-merges with es.json defaults
- Pages still work with raw JSON imports; overrides add on top dynamically

**Next things for you to decide:**
1. **Build the admin content editor** (El Viajero-style tree editor) — full CRUD for pricing, FAQ, Instagram, phone, email, SEO fields
2. **Wire `ContentProvider`** into pages so admin edits reflect live (currently the infrastructure is ready, pages use the JSON directly)
3. **Add more fields to Supabase overrides** — phone, WhatsApp links, pricing, coverage area

{"content": "FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
RUN apk add --no-cache libc6-compat
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm install --legacy-peer-deps

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
RUN apk add --no-cache wget curl
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && \
    adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=40s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:3000/ || exit 1
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
CMD node server.js
", "path": "/root/depiflash/Dockerfile"}

{"content": "     1|# DepiFlash — Complete Upgrade Plan
     2|
     3|**Goal:** Move DepiFlash from hardcoded JSON content + minimal admin to a full Supabase-backed content management system matching El Viajero's patterns, with an admin editor for all business content.
     4|
     5|## Architecture
     6|
     7|```
     8|content/es.json (defaults)  ──┐
     9|                               ├── ContentProvider (merge) ──→ pages
    10|ej_site_config (overrides)   ──┘
    11|     ↑
    12|  /api/admin/content (PUT)
    13|     ↑
    14|  Admin Editor (tree view)
    15|```
    16|
    17|## Phase 1: Content System (Supabase Backend)
    18|
    19|**Goal:** Every piece of business data is editable via Supabase, not code.
    20|
    21|### 1a. Seed all content to Supabase
    22|- Read every field from `content/es.json`
    23|- Store as full `content_overrides` in `ej_site_config`
    24|- Makes admin the source of truth; JSON becomes just a fallback
    25|
    26|### 1b. Public content API
    27|- `/api/content/overrides` — returns content_overrides JSONB
    28|- `/api/content/path?key=home.hero.headline` — gets specific path
    29|- Use service role key for reads too (trusted server)
    30|
    31|### 1c. ContentProvider (client-side)
    32|- Already created but pages DON'T use it yet
    33|- Must refactor every page to use `useContent()` instead of raw JSON import
    34|- Footer already wired
    35|
    36|## Phase 2: Admin Content Editor
    37|
    38|**Goal:** Dan can edit prices, FAQ, phone, WhatsApp link, Instagram, SEO from a web UI.
    39|
    40|### 2a. Admin auth
    41|- Supabase email/password login page (already exists at `/login`)
    42|- AdminGuard component protecting `/admin/*`
    43|- `/api/admin/auth` for session verification
    44|
    45|### 2b. Content editor
    46|- Tree editor component showing all sections
    47|- Editable fields: pricing (9 zones), FAQ (9 items), testimonials, SEO fields
    48|- Phone, WhatsApp link, Instagram, email, coverage area
    49|- Save button → PUT to `/api/admin/content`
    50|- Confirmation toast + optimistic UI
    51|
    52|### 2c. Admin dashboard
    53|- Google Analytics summary (sessions, page views, top pages)
    54|- Content last-edited timestamp
    55|- Quick links to WhatsApp
    56|
    57|## Phase 3: Page Refactoring
    58|
    59|**Goal:** All pages read from ContentProvider, not hardcoded JSON.
    60|
    61|### 3a. Layout refactor
    62|- Wrap `ContentProvider` in root layout
    63|- Update `page-meta.tsx` to use content from context
    64|
    65|### 3b. Inner pages
    66|- `servicios/page.tsx` — prices editable via admin
    67|- `como-funciona/page.tsx` — steps text editable
    68|- `faq/page.tsx` — questions/answers editable
    69|- `contacto/page.tsx` — phone, Instagram, email from content
    70|- `home/page.tsx` — hero, benefits, testimonials, CTA text editable
    71|
    72|### 3c. Remove hardcoded values
    73|- Phone `+595****2025` → all references use `content.whatsapp`
    74|- WhatsApp links with prefilled messages → construct from `content.whatsapp` + URL-encoded message
    75|- Coverage area → `content.coverage`
    76|- Business name, tagline → read from content
    77|
    78|## Phase 4: Analytics & Rate Limiting
    79|
    80|### 4a. Rate limit middleware
    81|- Token-bucket rate limiter on `/api/contact` and `/api/subscribe`
    82|- Memory-based, resets hourly
    83|
    84|### 4b. Admin analytics
    85|- Vercel Analytics data via their API
    86|- Simple dashboard cards (today's sessions, this week, top referral)
    87|
    88|## Phase 5: Deployment
    89|
    90|### 5a. Docker Swarm
    91|- Verify `.env` has all Supabase vars on VPS
    92|- Deploy new version with `docker stack deploy`
    93|
    94|### 5b. CI/CD
    95|- GitHub Actions already builds
    96|- On merge to main → auto-deploy via deploy-vps workflow
    97|
    98|---
    99|
   100|## Priority Order
   101|
   102|```
   103|Phase 1a ──→ Phase 1b ──→ Phase 1c ──→ Phase 3 ──→ Phase 2 ──→ Phase 4 ──→ Phase 5
   104|             (API)       (Provider)   (Pages)    (Editor)   (Limiting)
   105|```
   106|
   107|**Actually: Phase 2 (admin editor) comes before Phase 3 (page refactoring).**
   108|The page refactoring is only useful if the admin editor exists. Priority:
   109|
   110|1. Phase 1a — Seed all content to Supabase ✅ (partially — Instagram only)
   111|2. Phase 1b — API layer
   112|3. Phase 2 — Admin content editor
   113|4. Phase 3 — Refactor pages to use ContentProvider
   114|5. Phase 4 — Rate limiting & analytics
   115|6. Phase 5 — Deploy
   116|
   117|## Errors to Watch
   118|
   119|| Risk | Mitigation |
   120||------|------------|
   121|| Supabase service role key exposed client-side | Use server-only `/api/` routes |
   122|| ContentProvider flash of unmerged content | Show loading skeleton until merge complete |
   123|| Admin edit breaks JSON structure | Validate JSONB before writing |
   124|| Phone number formatting inconsistency | Single source: `content.whatsapp` everywhere |
   125|
   126|## Completion Criteria
   127|
   128|- [ ] All business data lives in Supabase `ej_site_config.content_overrides`
   129|- [ ] Admin editor at `/admin/content` can edit prices, FAQ, SEO, contacts
   130|- [ ] Every page reads from ContentProvider (not hardcoded JSON/strings)
   131|- [ ] No hardcoded phone, WhatsApp links, Instagram, or email in any component
   132|- [ ] Rate limiting on contact form
   133|- [ ] Deployed and verified live
   134|", "total_lines": 133, "file_size": 4725, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

En DeboraLaser, ofrecemos precios competitivos y transparentes para todos nuestros tratamientos de depilación láser en Cádiz y provincia. El coste de tu tratamiento dependerá de varios factores:

- Los packs de ahorro o promociones vigentes
- El número de sesiones necesarias según tu tipo de piel y vello
- El sexo (diferentes densidades y áreas de vello)
- La zona o zonas a tratar (facial, corporal, íntima)

      "url": "https://www.laesera.com/en/blogs/journal/hoe-lang-mag-je-niet-in-de-zon-na-ipl",
      "title": "Sunbathing after IPL: how long should you stay out of the sun? – Laesera",
      "content": "# Sunbathing after IPL: Comprehensive Summary

## Key Excerpts

> "IPL stands for Intense Pulsed Light. The device emits short flashes of light. This light is primarily absorbed by melanin, the pigment in your hair root and partly in your skin."

> "After a treatment, your skin is temporarily more sensitive to UV light, making it more susceptible to: hyperpigmentation (dark spots), hypopigmentation (lighter spots), redness or even a kind of 'sunburn reaction'."

> "The question 'how long to avoid the sun after IPL?' can practically be read as: 48 hours: strict avoidance, 1–2 weeks: no deliberate sun exposure, up to 4 weeks: going out in the sun, but with consistent protection."

## Why IPL + Sun is Problematic

- IPL light is absorbed by melanin in hair roots and skin
- After treatment, skin is temporarily more UV-sensitive
- Risks: hyperpigmentation, hypopigmentation, redness, sunburn-like reactions

## Three-Phase Sun Avoidance Timeline

### Phase 1: First 48 Hours – Strict Avoidance
- **No:** sunbathing, tanning beds, direct sunlight, sauna/steam bath
- **If going outside:** cover skin with clothing, use SPF 30–50 on exposed areas

### Phase 2: 1–2 Weeks – No Deliberate Sun Exposure
- **No:** targeted sunbathing, beach/pool sessions, tanning beds
- **Yes:** normal outdoor activities with:
  - Daily SPF 30–50 on treated areas
  - Reapply every 2 hours for prolonged exposure
  - Seek shade
  - Wear protective clothing (long dress, airy trousers, kaftan, hat)

### Phase 3: Up to 4 Weeks – Careful Protection
- Always protect treated skin with SPF 30–50
- No tanning beds
- Extra vigilance in strong sunlight (holidays, tropical destinations)

## Quick Reference Table

| Period | Avoid | Possible (with care) |
|--------|-------|---------------------|
| 0–48 hours | Tanning bed, sunbathing, sauna, hot baths | Indoors or outdoors with protective clothing |
| 2–14 days | Beach days, prolonged sunbathing | Outdoors with SPF 30–50 + shade |
| 2–4 weeks | Tanning bed, "baking" in full sun | Normal activities, always apply sunscreen |

## IPL in Summer – Practical Tips

- **Best timing:** Start in spring or autumn to avoid peak summer treatment phase
- **If starting in summer:**
  - Choose easily-covered areas first (bikini line, armpits)
  - Be extra careful with legs and arms
  - Plan sessions in the evening for overnight recovery
- Some brands claim IPL works with light tanning if sunscreen is used, but basic UV protection rules still apply

## Before & After IPL Checklist

### Before Treatment (1–2 weeks prior)
- No tanning bed
- No deliberate sunbathing on treatment area
- No self-tanner on skin
- Ensure skin is not sunburned or flaky
- Use mild skincare without harsh acids

### After Treatment
- Cool and soothe with gentle, fragrance-free cream/gel
- First 24–48 hours: avoid intense exercise, sauna, steam bath, hot baths
- No scratching, scrubbing, or picking
- Daily SPF 30–50 on treated areas exposed to daylight

## When to Pause or Seek Professional Help

Stop treatment and consult a professional if experiencing:
- Severe redness, blisters, or signs of burning
- New, noticeable dark or light spots on treated area
- Persistent pain, tightness, or warmth

## Laesera Nova One Recommendations

- Build a fixed routine tailored to your skin and schedule
- Plan treatments in spring for summer benefits
- In summer, treat mainly easily-covered areas
- Always follow device manual safety instructions
- Consider skin type and any medical conditions
- Consult doctor for medication or skin conditions

## Final Takeaway

> "Sun exposure after IPL requires conscious handling of UV light. The combination of IPL and sun is not forbidden, but requires planning."

Safe IPL in summer is possible with:
- Consistent SPF protection
- Protective clothing when needed
- Following device guidelines and respecting your skin's needs",

      "title": "Guía de Depilación Láser en Inglés: Brasileñas, Normales e Integrales",
      "content": "# Guía de Depilación Láser en Inglés: Brasileñas, Normales e Integrales

**Fuente:** [No+Vello](https://www.nomasvello.es/[REDACTED_TOKEN]/)

---

## Resumen General

La depilación láser es una solución duradera para el vello no deseado en la zona de las ingles. Existen tres estilos principales: **normales**, **brasileñas** e **integrales**. La elección depende de preferencias personales, tolerancia al dolor y estilo de vida.

### Cómo funciona la depilación láser
> "La depilación láser utiliza un rayo de luz altamente concentrado que penetra los folículos pilosos. La energía lumínica es absorbida por la melanina en el pelo, destruyendo el folículo y reduciendo el crecimiento futuro."

**Beneficios clave:**
- Mejora la textura de la piel y reduce el vello encarnado
- Menos propensión a irritaciones y problemas cutáneos
- Reducción del vello a largo plazo vs. métodos tradicionales (cera, afeitado)

---

## Tipos de Depilación Láser de Ingles

### 1. Ingles Normales
- **Enfoque:** Línea del bikini, elimina vello que sobresale de la ropa interior
- **Ideal para:** Aspecto natural pero limpio

| **Ventajas** | **Desventajas** |
|--------------|-----------------|
| Menos dolor (menos área tratada) | Menor cobertura (deja más vello) |
| Apariencia más natural | |
| Menos tiempo de tratamiento y sesiones | |

### 2. Ingles Brasileñas
- **Enfoque:** Elimina más vello que las normales, dejando una franja estrecha en la parte delantera y eliminando todo el vello circundante
- **Ideal para:** Diferentes estilos de ropa interior y bañadores

| **Ventajas** | **Desventajas** |
|--------------|-----------------|
| Versatilidad | Mayor dolor (área de tratamiento mayor) |
| Estética más definida y limpia | Más tiempo de tratamiento y sesiones |

### 3. Ingles Integrales
- **Enfoque:** Elimina todo el vello de la zona púbica, dejando la piel completamente suave
- **Ideal para:** Quienes prefieren una estética completamente sin vello

| **Ventajas** | **Desventajas** |
|--------------|-----------------|
| Limpieza total | Requiere compromiso a largo plazo y mantenimiento estricto |
| Máxima suavidad | Puede ser más doloroso |

---

## Recomendaciones Clave

> "Antes del estilo de depilación que decidas escoger, es recomendable realizar una evaluación de la piel y el vello con un especialista."

**Preparación:**
- Mantener la piel libre de bronceado (no exponerse al sol antes del tratamiento)
- La zona de aplicación debe estar afeitada

**Tecnología:**
> "La tecnología láser ha avanzado para ser segura y efectiva en una amplia variedad de tipos de piel, aunque la consulta inicial es crucial para determinar la mejor opción para cada individuo."

---

## Decisión Final

| **Si buscas...** | **Elige...** |
|------------------|--------------|
| Look natural | Ingles normales |
| Mayor definición | Ingles brasileñas |
| Limpieza total | Ingles integrales |

---

## Información de Contacto

**Teléfonos:** [REDACTED_PHONE]  
**Email:** [REDACTED_EMAIL]

**Servicios ofrecidos:**

- Método Fusion
- Cosmética pre-post depilación
- Tratamientos faciales (rejuvenecimiento IPL, limpieza ProSkin)
- Promociones y tienda online

**Nota:** Financiado por la Unión Europea - NextGenerationEU",

{"content": "# DepiFlash — Dan's Enhancement Batch Plan (May 2026)

## Overview

4 changes from Dan's WhatsApp messages on May 20-21, 2026:

1. **Synonym for "Inglés completo"** → change to standard term
2. **Electrolysis research** → add electrolysis as complementary service?  
3. **Sun exposure advice fix** → replace "no te expongas al sol 48 horas antes" with correct advice
4. **Color scheme** → pink pastel + feminine elegant palette

---

## Batch 1: Content & Terminology

### 1a. "Inglés completo" → "Zona íntima completa"

**Research findings:**
- "Inglés completo" is outdated slang in Paraguay
- Clinics use: "Zona íntima completa", "Bikini completo", "Depilación integral"
- Most common in LATAM: **"Zona íntima completa"** (cepilarte, Piel&Co)
- For the pricing zone: `{ name: "Zona íntima completa", small: false, medium: true, ... }`

**Files to change:**
- `content/es.json` — zone name line 72, FAQ answer lines 100, 119
- API route — no change needed (reads from content overrides)
- Admin editor — no change needed (reads from content)

### 1b. Sun exposure advice — CORRECT medical guideline

**Research findings (BMLA, Laesera, StatPearls):**

| Current text (WRONG) | Correct text |
|---------------------|--------------|
| "No te expongas al sol 48 horas antes" | "No te expongas la zona a tratar al sol DIRECTAMENTE los días previos a la sesión. Si estuviste al sol, esperá al menos 1-2 semanas antes de la sesión." |
| Missing post-care | "Después de la sesión: no te expongas la zona tratada al sol por 48 horas. Usá protector solar SPF 50+ si vas a estar al aire libre." |

**Files to change:**
- `content/es.json` — step 2 description line 54, FAQ items line 99
- `servicios/page.tsx` — prep section hardcoded text (line ~65-75)

### 1c. Add relevant FAQ about solar exposure before/after

Add a dedicated FAQ item that answers proactively.

---

## Batch 2: Electrolysis Research Integration

**Research findings:**
- **Electrolysis vs IPL**: electrolysis is slower (10-30 min per small area), more painful, more expensive (18-35€ per session), but works on ALL hair types (including blonde/white). IPL is faster, cheaper per session, less painful, but only works on dark hair.
- **Market in Paraguay**: No major electrolysis providers found in Paraguay search. Main players are IPL/laser (DepilArte, Piel&Co, Espaçolaser, Dermalaser). **Electrolysis is niche** — mostly used for small areas (facial, eyebrows) and for clients with light/blonde hair that IPL can't treat.
- **Recommendation**: Don't offer electrolysis as a service. Instead:
  1. Mention in FAQ that IPL works on dark hair; for very light/blonde we can discuss alternatives
  2. Position IPL as superior for most clients (larger areas, less pain, better value)

**Files to change:**
- No code changes needed — just note this for Dan's business strategy

---

## Batch 3: Color Scheme Overhaul

### Current scheme
- Primary: `#E8795B` (salmon/coral) — warm, somewhat feminine but neutral
- Secondary: `#2DD4BF` (teal/teal) — gradient partner
- Background: `#FFF1EE`, `#FFFBFA`, `#FDF2F8` (light pinks and peaches)

### New scheme — "Rose Elegance"
Based on Dan's requirements (pastel pink + feminine + elegant):

| Usage | Current | New | Hex |
|-------|---------|-----|-----|
| Primary button/CTA | `#E8795B` | Rose | `#E8A0BF` |
| Primary hover | `#d4684e` | Rose darker | `#D484A8` |
| Secondary gradient partner | `#2DD4BF` | Lavender | `#C4A4D4` |
| Hero/text accent | `#E8795B` | Dusty rose | `#D4A0A0` |
| Light bg 1 | `#FFF1EE` | Pink ice | `#FFF0F5` |
| Light bg 2 | `#FFFBFA` | Lavender mist | `#F8F0FF` |
| Light bg 3 | `#FDF2F8` | Rose water | `#FFF0F0` |
| Text headings | `#1A1A2E` | Charcoal | Keep `#1A1A2E` |
| Button text | `#FFFFFF` | White | Keep `#FFFFFF` |

### Files to change (color references):
1. `tailwind.config.js` — add new colors
2. `app/globals.css` — if custom CSS
3. `app/page.tsx` — inline styles (gradients, backgrounds)
4. `app/servicios/page.tsx` — inline styles
5. `app/como-funciona/page.tsx` — inline styles
6. `app/contacto/page.tsx` — inline styles
7. `app/faq/page.tsx` — inline styles
8. `components/header.tsx` — any custom colors
9. `components/footer.tsx` — custom colors
10. `components/whatsapp-float.tsx` — custom colors
11. `components/mobile-cta.tsx` — custom colors
12. `components/cta-banner.tsx` — custom colors

### Key color replacement table
```
#E8795B → #E8A0BF  (primary rose)
#d4684e → #D484A8  (primary hover)
#2DD4BF → #C4A4D4  (secondary accent)
#FFF1EE → #FFF0F5  (bg light pink)
#FFFBFA → #F8F0FF  (bg lavender mist)
#FDF2F8 → #FFF0F0  (bg rose water)
```

The hero gradient changes from:
```
linear-gradient(135deg, #FFF1EE 0%, #FDF2F8 50%, #ECFDF5 100%)
→
linear-gradient(135deg, #FFF0F5 0%, #FFF0F0 50%, #F8F0FF 100%)
```

The CTA banner changes from:
```
linear-gradient(135deg, #E8795B 0%, #2DD4BF 100%)
→
linear-gradient(135deg, #E8A0BF 0%, #C4A4D4 100%)
```

---

## Execution Order

```
Batch 1a (terminology) ──→ Batch 1b (sun advice) ──→ Batch 3 (colors)
```

Batch 2 (electrolysis research) is strategy-only, no code.

---

## Timing
- Content/terminology changes: ~10 min (JSON edits only)
- Sun exposure changes: ~20 min (content JSON + FAQ + component hardcoded text)
- Color scheme: ~30 min (find-replace across ~12 files)
- **Total: ~ 1 hour**

Line 73 has the WRONG sun advice — "NO te expongas al sol 48 horas antes". The correct advice is about **after treatment** not before. Let me fix:

function.name: patch
function.arguments:
{"new_string": "

❌ Si estuviste al sol los días previos, avisame — podemos reprogramar. Idealmente esperá una semana entre exposición solar y sesión.

✅ Después de la sesión: no te expongas la zona tratada al sol por 48 horas y usá protector solar SPF 50+.

❌ No uses cremas, lociones, desodorante ni maquillaje en la zona el día de la sesión.

", "old_string": "

❌ NO te expongas al sol 48 horas antes.

", "path": "/root/depiflash/app/servicios/page.tsx"}

messages.content:
{"success": true, "diff": "--- a//root/depiflash/app/servicios/page.tsx +++ b//root/depiflash/app/servicios/page.tsx @@ -70,7 +70,8 @@

✅ Afeitá la zona 24 horas antes — es el paso más importante.

✅ Lavá la zona con agua y jabón neutro el día de la sesión.

❌ NO uses cera, crema depilatoria ni pinzas 2 semanas antes.

-

❌ NO te expongas al sol 48 horas antes.

+

❌ Si estuviste al sol los días previos, avisame — podemos reprogramar. Idealmente esperá una semana entre exposición solar y sesión.

+

{"content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Sync depiflash content to Supabase — reflect the JSON changes"""
import json, urllib.request

SUPABASE_URL = "[REDACTED_SUPABASE_URL]"
SERVICE_KEY = "[REDACTED_SUPABASE_KEY]"

with open("/root/depiflash/content/es.json") as f:
    content = json.load(f)

# Only sync the fields that changed
overrides = {
    "home.pricing.zones[8].name": "Zona íntima completa",
    "home.pricing.zones[8].medium": True,
    "home.faq.items[2].answer": content["home"]["faq"]["items"][2]["answer"],
    "home.faq.items[9]": content["home"]["faq"]["items"][9],
    "faq.items[2].answer": content["faq"]["items"][2]["answer"],
    "home.howItWorks.steps[1].description": content["home"]["howItWorks"]["steps"][1]["description"],
}

payload = json.dumps(overrides, ensure_ascii=False).encode("utf-8")
req = urllib.request.Request(
    f"{SUPABASE_URL}/rest/v1/ej_site_config?key=eq.content_overrides",
    data=payload,
    headers={
        "apikey": SERVICE_KEY,
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {SERVICE_KEY}",
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
        "Prefer": "return=minimal",
    },
    method="PATCH",
)
with urllib.request.urlopen(req) as resp:
    print(f"Status: {resp.status}")
    print("Done")
", "path": "/tmp/sync-content.py"}
